summary: Soaring Dark Star→Morning Dew sandwich, tight first‑set groove
blurb: The Spectrum show delivers a high‑energy first set that rolls into a monumental second set. Highlights include the Dark Star → Morning Dew sandwich, a fiery Playing In The Band, and crisp renditions of Bird Song and China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider. Jerry’s lead work and Phil’s vocal presence keep the band locked in throughout.
review: September 21, 1972 at the Spectrum captures one of the Dead’s most exhilarating nights of the early‑’70s. The first set launches with a bright Bird Song and rolls through tight classics like China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, Jack Straw and Cumberland Blues, all delivered with crisp harmonies and a propulsive groove. The second set erupts after a teasing He's Gone → Truckin' segue into a blistering Dark Star that blossoms into an unforgettable Morning Dew sandwich, bookended by a thunderous Playing In The Band and a jubilant Sugar Magnolia. Jerry Garcia’s guitar soars—especially on the Dark Star jam—and his vocal phrasing on Morning Dew feels especially heartfelt. Phil Lesh adds depth with clear, punchy bass tones and memorable vocal moments, while Bob Weir’s “nailed” vocals shine on Jack Straw. The band stays locked in from start to finish, making this a must‑listen performance for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the Dark Star → Morning Dew sandwich, Playing In The Band, and overall tightness of the performance. Multiple sources highlight Jerry’s lead work, Phil’s bass tone, and the high‑energy second set, indicating strong consensus on exceptional playing.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard mix that captures the full show with excellent detail and minimal tape noise, delivering the best listening experience.

key_highlights: Dark Star → Morning Dew sandwich in the second set
Explosive Playing In The Band leading into the sandwich
Tight first‑set groove with Bird Song and China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
High‑energy second set that keeps listeners hooked throughout

band_performance.Jerry: Leads the Dark Star jam, delivers a beautiful spin on Morning Dew, and his guitar tone is rich and central to the performance; visibly pleased throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Provides strong vocal contributions (notably on Cumberland Blues), memorable intro banter, and his bass tone is clear, close‑miked and adds depth to the mix.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals are "nailed" on Jack Straw with great harmonies; rhythm guitar work solid and supportive.
